business_justification: ESXi host runs 15 production VMs including customer-data-api and analytics-pipeline. Patch requires full host evacuation — vMotion bandwidth insufficient for simultaneous migration. Sequential migration requires 8-hour window. Next available extended window: January 2026.
compensating_controls: 1. ESXi management interface restricted to management VLAN (10.70.100.0/24) with ACL permitting only jump-box-05 and vcenter-prod. 2. CrowdStrike Falcon on all 15 guest VMs with behavioral detection and network containment. 3. Palo Alto IDS/IPS signatures for CVE-2025-22224 (Threat ID 95823). 4. VMware Tools guest isolation enabled. 5. Automated VM snapshots every 6 hours.
review_notes: Risk acceptance approved June 20. Initial validation completed same day by Security Operations. CRITICAL: No quarterly revalidation has been performed. Next revalidation was due September 18, 2025 (90 days after initial validation). As of December 29, 2025, revalidation is 102 days overdue. Per policy Section 5.3, risk acceptances with validations overdue by 90+ days are considered void. Additionally, the risk acceptance itself expired on December 20, 2025 (6-month maximum) and has not been renewed. Compensating control status is unknown — last verified June 20, over 6 months ago.

business_justification: Internal firewall in HA cluster. Patch requires HA failover causing 30-60 second traffic disruption. Business stakeholders repeatedly defer maintenance window approval due to uptime requirements.
compensating_controls: 1. FortiGate management interface restricted to management VLAN — not reachable from production or corporate networks. 2. Admin access requires MFA via FortiToken hardware tokens. 3. Local administrator accounts disabled — RADIUS authentication to Okta with MFA enforced. 4. Network IDS monitoring management VLAN for CVE-2025-24472 exploitation indicators. 5. FortiManager audit logging enabled with Splunk forwarding — all admin actions logged.
review_notes: Initial validation July 8 by Security Operations. Q3 revalidation completed October 8 by Maria Santos (Security Operations): all 5 controls verified operational — management VLAN ACLs confirmed, FortiToken MFA tested, local admin disabled (show system admin output reviewed), IDS signatures active (0 hits), FortiManager logs flowing to Splunk (sample log reviewed). Next revalidation due January 6, 2026. Note: Business stakeholders have deferred the HA failover maintenance window 3 times (originally scheduled for September, then October, then November). Current target: January 11, 2026.

business_justification: Legacy billing application on Struts 2.3.x. Full rewrite required — estimated 5 months. Application retirement approved for H1 2026.
compensating_controls: 1. Application not internet-facing — internal VPN access only. 2. WAF (Imperva) with Struts exploit signatures. 3. Application service account has read-only DB permissions. 4. Enhanced request logging via Splunk.
review_notes: Initial validation June 5. Q3 revalidation completed September 5 — all controls verified. Risk acceptance expired December 5 (6-month maximum). No renewal filed. Vulnerability remains open. Application retirement timeline unchanged (H1 2026) but formal risk coverage has lapsed.

business_justification: D-Link NAS — end of life, no vendor patch. Non-critical archive storage. Hardware replacement in Q1 2026 budget.
compensating_controls: 1. Isolated VLAN — no internet access. 2. Firewall ACL restricts to 4 named users. 3. No PII or regulated data. 4. Weekly S3 replication.
review_notes: Initial validation May 18. Q3 revalidation August 18. Risk acceptance expired November 18. Not renewed — team assumes replacement in Q1 covers remaining period. However, no formal risk acceptance is active.
